sections.paragraph:
κύω impf. 1 pl. ἐκύομεν Is 59:13; fut. 2 pl. κυήσεσθε Is 33:11 Sym.; aor. 3 sg. (Att.) ἐκύησεν (Mel., P. 52, 386); aor. pass. ptc. n. pl. κυηθέντων (Ath., R. 17 p. 69, 8) (later form of κυέω; Hom. Hymns et al.; PSI 167, 19 [II b.c.]; Is 59:4, 13; Philo; Joseph.) conceive, become pregnant (Jos., Ant. 1, 257) τῷ στόματι through the mouth, of the weasel B 10:8 (for the idea s. Windisch, Hdb. ad loc., and the passages given under γαλῆ; also TDuncan, The Weasel in Myths, Superstition and Religion: Washington Univ. Studies, Humanistic Ser. XII 1925, 33ff).—DELG s.v. κυέω I.

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. pl. = plural

fut. fut. = fut.

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 579
